author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm XVI

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The inscription of the title, of David himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Our King in this Psalm speaks in the character of the human nature He assumed, of whom the royal title at the time of His passion was eminently set forth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now He saith as follows; “Preserve me, O Lord, for in Thee have I hoped” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 1): “I have said to the Lord, Thou art my God, for Thou requirest not my goods” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 2): for with my goods Thou dost not look to be made blessed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “To the saints who are on His earth” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 3): to the saints who have placed their hope in the land of the living, the citizens of the heavenly Jerusalem, whose spiritual conversation is, by the anchor of hope, fixed in that country, which is rightly called God’s earth; although as yet in this earth too they be conversant in the flesh.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He hath wonderfully fulfilled all My wishes in them.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To those saints then He hath wonderfully fulfilled all My wishes in their advancement, whereby they have perceived, how both the humanity of My divinity hath profited them that I might die, and the divinity of the humanity that I might rise again.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Their infirmities have been multiplied” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 4): their infirmities have been multiplied not for their destruction, but that they might long for the Physician.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Afterwards they made haste.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Accordingly after infirmities multiplied they made haste, that they might be healed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will not gather together their assemblies by blood.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For their assemblies shall not be carnal, nor will I gather them together as one propitiated by the blood of cattle.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Nor will I be mindful of their names within My lips.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But by a spiritual change what they have been shall be forgotten; nor by Me shall they be any more called either sinners, or enemies, or men; but righteous, and My brethren, and sons of God through My peace.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord is the portion of Mine inheritance, and of My cup” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For together with Me they shall possess the inheritance, the Lord Himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let others choose for themselves portions, earthly and temporal, to enjoy: the portion of the Saints is the Lord eternal.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let others drink of deadly pleasures, the portion of My cup is the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In that I say, “Mine,” I include the Church: for where the Head is, there is the body also.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For into the inheritance will I gather together their assemblies, and by the inebriation of the cup I will forget their old names.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou art He who will restore to Me My inheritance:” that to these too, whom I free, may be known “the glory wherein I was with Thee before the world was made.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For Thou wilt not restore to Me that which I never lost, but Thou wilt restore to these, who have lost it, the knowledge of that glory: in whom because I am, Thou wilt restore to Me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The lines have fallen to me in glorious places” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The boundaries of my possession have fallen in Thy glory as it were by lot, like as God is the possession of the Priests and Levites.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For Mine inheritance is glorious to Me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For Mine inheritance is glorious,” not to all, but to them that see; in whom because I am, “it is to Me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will bless the Lord, who hath given Me understanding” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 7): whereby this inheritance may be seen and possessed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Yea moreover too even unto night my reins have chastened Me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yea besides understanding, even unto death, My inferior part, the assumption of flesh, hath instructed Me, that I might experience the darkness of mortality, which that understanding hath not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I foresaw the Lord in My sight always” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But coming into things that pass away, I removed not Mine eye from Him who abideth ever, foreseeing this, that to Him I should return after passing through the things temporal.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For He is on My right hand, that I should not be moved.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He favoureth Me, that I should abide fixedly in Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Wherefore My heart was glad, and My tongue exulted” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore both in My thoughts is gladness, and in my words exultation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Moreover too My flesh shall rest in hope.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Moreover too My flesh shall not fail unto destruction, but shall sleep in hope of the resurrection.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For Thou wilt not leave My soul in hell” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For Thou wilt neither give My soul for a possession to those parts below.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Neither wilt Thou grant Thine Holy One to see corruption.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Neither wilt Thou suffer that sanctified body, whereby others are to be also sanctified, to see corruption.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast made known to Me the paths of life” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast made known through Me the paths of humiliation, that men might return to life, from whence they fell through pride; in whom because I am, “Thou hast made known to Me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou wilt fill Me with joy with Thy countenance.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou wilt fill them with joy, that they should seek nothing further, when they shall see Thee “face to face;” in whom because I am, “Thou wilt fill Me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Pleasure is at Thy right hand even to the end.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Pleasure is in Thy favour and mercy in this life’s journey, leading on even to the end of the glory of Thy countenance.
